turns.content: What brings you in today?
Well, doc, I've been feeling just plain worn out lately. No matter how much sleep I get, I'm still tired. And I've noticed I'm losing weight without really trying.
When did this start?
I'm not sure exactly. Maybe a few months back? The tiredness just sort of snuck up on me. The weight loss I've noticed more in the last couple months.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?
I don't really have a sharp pain or anything. It's more like a heavy, tired feeling all over, especially in my arms and legs. Like I've been scrubbing floors all day even when I've just woken up.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, nothing like that. Just the tiredness and the weight. No fevers or chills that I can remember.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take a little white pill in the morning for my blood pressure, and a bigger one at night. That's all. I don't remember the names.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
Just the high blood pressure, as far as I know. That's what the morning pill is for.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I don't think so. I've never had a problem with any medicine before.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?

Not really. I just figured I needed more rest, so I tried sleeping longer. But it didn't help. A friend said maybe I needed more iron, so I bought some from the store, but I didn't notice much difference.
Does anything make it better or worse?
Work makes it worse, that's for sure. By the end of my shift I can barely keep going. Resting doesn't really make it better, like I said. I just stay tired.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's been hard. I used to get through my cleaning shifts without trouble, but now I drag myself around and have to take more breaks. My boss has noticed, and I'm worried I might lose hours. I just don't have the get-up-and-go I used to.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
No, I never smoked, and I only have a glass of wine at Christmas or a family party. Nothing regular.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?
I don't really know. My mother had heart trouble, and my father passed from cancer in his stomach. But nobody mentioned being tired like this.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, never. I've always been a hard worker and slept fine. This is new for me.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?
Yes, I think so. Whatever you think is best, doc. I just want to feel like myself again.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?
Physically? I'd say maybe a 7. It's wearing me down pretty bad. Emotionally... I'm a little scared, maybe a 6 or 7. I don't like not knowing what's wrong with me.
Do you have any questions for me?
No. Just... do you think it's something serious? And is there anything I can take to get my energy back?
What do you think might be going on?
I don't know, doc. That's why I'm here. You're the expert. I just hope it's nothing too bad.
Is there anything else you think I should know?
I don't think so. Just... I had a dizzy spell at work last week, but it passed quick. And my husband says my skin looks kind of pale, but I thought it was just the light in our kitchen.
